text.title: Quality Attributes Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the quality-attributes methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every NFR MUST be captured as an ATAM-style scenario: Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artefact, Response, Measure. Bare adjectives ('fast', 'secure') MUST be rejected.

rationale.source: Bass / Clements / Kazman — Software Architecture in Practice.

rule.rationale: The 6-field shape forces numeric Response and Measure; adjectives cannot be tested.

rule.statement: Spec MUST cover Performance, Reliability, Security, Maintainability, Scalability, Usability, and Compliance categories (omit a category only when explicitly out of scope, with reason).
rule.rationale: ISO 25010 is the de-facto NFR taxonomy; gaps usually mean the attribute is implicit and gets misaligned.

rule.statement: Scenarios MUST be prioritised in a utility tree (high / medium / low importance × high / medium / low difficulty). High-importance × low-difficulty scenarios MUST be implemented first; high × high require an ATAM session.
rule.rationale: Without explicit prioritisation every scenario looks equal and engineering capacity is mis-allocated.

rule.statement: Each high-priority scenario MUST be translated into one Service Level Indicator with a numeric SLO target; SLOs MUST be testable in CI / production monitoring.
rule.rationale: Scenarios live in a doc; SLIs/SLOs live in monitoring; without translation the NFR is never enforced.

rule.statement: Spec MUST name at least 2 cross-attribute conflicts (e.g. perf vs cost, security vs usability) and a resolution rule for each.
rule.rationale: Unnamed conflicts surface as ad-hoc decisions later; named conflicts produce stable, auditable trade-off rules.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (stakeholders, goals, current SLIs, compliance matrix), apply r1 → r2 → r3 → r4 → r5 and emit the spec.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: shape first, coverage second, prioritisation third, monitoring fourth, trade-offs last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) prototype / no contractual NFR, (b) internal CLI, (c) NFR spec already current. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: NFR ceremony in these contexts wastes review.
